name: Sausage and Mushroom Phyllo Twists
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, cook the sausage meat over moderately high heat, stirring and breaking up any larger pieces, until it is cooked through and browned.
2. Transfer the meat to a fine sieve, and drain. In the skillet, cook the onion and garlic in unsalted butter over moderately low heat until the onion is softened.
3. Add the mushrooms, and cook the mixture over moderate heat, stirring until the liquid evaporates from the mushrooms.
4. Add the cumin, allspice, and mint, and cook the mixture, stirring, for 2 minutes.
5. Transfer the mixture to a bowl, add the sausage meat, parsley, cream cheese, and season with salt and pepper.
6. Working quickly to keep the phyllo from drying out, place one sheet of phyllo on a work surface, brush lightly with clarified butter, and sprinkle with bread crumbs.
7. Lay another sheet of phyllo on top of the first sheet, brushing lightly with remaining clarified butter.
8. Cut the sheets into thirds lengthwise and into quarters crosswise to form 12 squares.
9. Place a rounded teaspoon of filling in each square, gather the corners of the phyllo over the filling, and twist gently to seal it.
10. Continue making hors d'oeuvres using remaining phyllo, butter, crumbs, and filling.
11. Bake the sausage and mushroom phyllo twists in jelly-roll pans in a preheated oven at 400 degrees for 10-12 minutes, or until the phyllo is golden brown.
